title: Bora Bora 14-Day Honeymoon Package
metaTitle: Bora Bora 14-Day Honeymoon Package | Flights, Hotels & Tours Included
metaDescription: Book a 14-day trip to Bora Bora, French Polynesia: round-trip flights, hotel, breakfast, transfers, and guided tours including Lagoon shark & ray feeding snorkel tour and Mount Otemanu 4x4 safari. Prices from $5452 per person (estimate).
highlights: Lagoon shark & ray feeding snorkel tour
Overwater bungalow with a glass floor and private plunge pool
Overwater villa with direct lagoon access
overview: Spend 14 days discovering Bora Bora, French Polynesia — Stable tropical climate with warm temperatures year-round (27-31°C); a drier, cooler season (May-Oct) and a warmer, more humid season with brief showers (Nov-Apr). May to October, the dry season, for lower humidity, calmer lagoon waters, and the best underwater visibility. This itinerary blends Lagoon shark & ray feeding snorkel tour, Mount Otemanu 4x4 safari, Matira Beach with free time to explore at your own pace, backed by round-trip flights, handpicked accommodation, and a dedicated travel consultant from enquiry through departure.
included: Round-trip economy-class airfare from your selected origin city (upgrade to premium economy or business available)
13 nights accommodation in your selected category (Budget, Mid-range, Premium, or Luxury)
Daily local transport as per the itinerary
Guided tours including Lagoon shark & ray feeding snorkel tour, Mount Otemanu 4x4 safari, Matira Beach
All entrance fees for attractions and activities listed in the itinerary
Applicable taxes and OTA service fee
Dedicated travel consultant support from booking through departure
notIncluded: Visa fees and travel document costs (see Visa Information)
Travel insurance (strongly recommended, available as an add-on)
Lunches and dinners except where noted in the itinerary
Optional activities not listed in the day-by-day itinerary
Airline seat selection and baggage upgrades
Any fare difference for premium economy or business class upgrades

itinerary.title: Arrive in Bora Bora
itinerary.description: Land at Bora Bora Airport (Motu Mute) (BOB) and take your included private transfer to your hotel. After settling in, ease into the trip with a relaxed visit to Lagoon shark & ray feeding snorkel tour — Guided snorkel with blacktip sharks and stingrays. Round off the evening with a welcome dinner introducing you to local flavors.
activities: Lagoon shark & ray feeding snorkel tour

itinerary.title: Bora Bora: Mount Otemanu 4x4 safari & Helicopter tour over the lagoon
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Mount Otemanu 4x4 safari — Off-road tour to the base of the extinct volcano. In the afternoon, continue to Helicopter tour over the lagoon — Aerial views of Bora Bora's famous turquoise lagoon. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Bora Bora: Bora Bora Jet Ski lagoon tour & Scuba diving with lemon sharks
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Bora Bora Jet Ski lagoon tour — Guided jet ski circuit around the island. In the afternoon, continue to Scuba diving with lemon sharks — Two-tank dive for certified divers. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.
activities: Bora Bora Jet Ski lagoon tour

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Tahiti (Papeete) day trip
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Tahiti (Papeete) day trip, a popular side trip from Bora Bora. Return to Bora Bora in the evening with time free to explore on your own.
activities: Guided day trip: Tahiti (Papeete) day trip

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Anau village — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.

itinerary.title: Bora Bora: Private lagoon picnic on a motu & Coral Garden snorkeling excursion
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Private lagoon picnic on a motu — Private islet picnic with a boat crew. In the afternoon, continue to Coral Garden snorkeling excursion — Guided snorkel over vibrant coral gardens. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.
activities: Private lagoon picnic on a motu

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Taha'a vanilla island
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Taha'a vanilla island, a popular side trip from Bora Bora. Return to Bora Bora in the evening with time free to explore on your own.
activities: Guided day trip: Taha'a vanilla island

itinerary.title: Bora Bora: Traditional Polynesian dance dinner show & Deep-sea fishing charter
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Traditional Polynesian dance dinner show — Cultural performance with a local feast. In the afternoon, continue to Deep-sea fishing charter — Half-day charter for mahi-mahi and tuna. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Faanui Bay WWII relics — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Moorea Island
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Moorea Island, a popular side trip from Bora Bora. Return to Bora Bora in the evening with time free to explore on your own.

itinerary.title: Bora Bora: Le Truck island tour & Matira Beach
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Le Truck island tour — Open-air shuttle tour around the main island. In the afternoon, continue to Matira Beach — Public white-sand beach on the southern tip. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Bora Bora: Vaitape village & Bloody Mary's restaurant grounds
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Vaitape village — Main town with market and shops. In the afternoon, continue to Bloody Mary's restaurant grounds — Famous thatched-roof restaurant with a celebrity guestbook. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Motu Tapu private islet — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.

itinerary.title: Departure Day
itinerary.description: Enjoy a final morning at leisure — perhaps one last visit to Faanui Bay or some souvenir shopping — before your private transfer to BOB for your departure flight.

travelTips: Book overwater bungalow resorts and airport boat transfers well in advance
Carry extra cash — many small vendors don't accept cards
Basic French phrases help beyond resort staff
Withdraw cash in Vaitape, as ATMs are scarce elsewhere on the island
Pace yourself — this itinerary includes free time so you are not rushing between every attraction.
packingList: Copies of booking confirmations and travel insurance
Lightweight, breathable clothing and a reusable water bottle
Reef-safe sunscreen to protect the lagoon's coral
Lightweight resort wear plus one modest outfit for village visits
A waterproof phone case for boat transfers and snorkeling
thingsToDo: Lagoon shark & ray feeding snorkel tour
Bora Bora Jet Ski lagoon tour
Private lagoon picnic on a motu
Scuba diving with lemon and blacktip sharks
thingsToAvoid: Touching or standing on coral while snorkeling
Expecting affordable dining outside resort meal packages
Skipping travel insurance given the remote location and limited medical facilities
Overpacking heavy luggage given small inter-island aircraft weight limits
topRestaurants: St James Restaurant — International and seafood ($$$)
Snack Ben's — Local casual snack bar ($)
instagramSpots: Overwater bungalow with Mount Otemanu backdrop
adventureActivities: Scuba diving with lemon and blacktip sharks
luxuryExperiences: Overwater bungalow with a glass floor and private plunge pool
honeymoonExperiences: Overwater villa with direct lagoon access
Private sunset dinner on a sandbank
Couples spa treatment over the water
budgetExperiences: Le Truck island tour for budget sightseeing
Picnic with supplies from the Vaitape market

festivalCalendar.name: Heiva i Bora Bora (traditional dance and sports)
Tahiti Pearl Regatta (passes near Bora Bora)
Bora Bora Liquid Festival (surf and SUP)

localEtiquette: Dress modestly when visiting Vaitape village outside the resort.
Respect the Polynesian concept of "mana," a spiritual reverence for nature and place.
Remove shoes when entering a local home.
Tipping is not customary in French Polynesia and can feel awkward if offered.
Ask before photographing local residents up close.
localTransportGuide: Bora Bora has no traffic lights and limited roads; most resorts sit on private motus reached by boat shuttle from the airport. Le Truck (open-air shuttle), bicycles, and golf carts are the main ways to explore Vaitape and the main island.
bestTimeToVisit: May to October, the dry season, for lower humidity, calmer lagoon waters, and the best underwater visibility.
weather: Stable tropical climate with warm temperatures year-round (27-31°C); a drier, cooler season (May-Oct) and a warmer, more humid season with brief showers (Nov-Apr).
visaInformation: As a French overseas collectivity, entry follows French rules: EU citizens travel freely, and most other tourist nationalities (US, UK, Canada, Australia) can enter visa-free for up to 90 days with a valid passport and onward ticket.
averageFlightTime: ~18.2h from major hubs (estimate; varies by origin — see Suggested Flights)
cancellationPolicy: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.
termsAndConditions: Pricing shown is per person based on the traveler type and tier selected and is an estimate pending live fare and rate confirmation.
Passports must be valid for at least 6 months beyond the return date for most destinations — confirm the exact requirement for your nationality.
Itinerary order may change due to weather, local conditions, or attraction closures; inclusions remain equivalent.
Solo travelers incur a single-room supplement, reflected in the Solo pricing tier.
This package does not include international travel insurance unless explicitly listed under Included.
Prices shown in a currency other than USD are approximate conversions at the rate noted and are not a payment quote.

faqs.question: Do I need a visa to visit Bora Bora?
faqs.answer: As a French overseas collectivity, entry follows French rules: EU citizens travel freely, and most other tourist nationalities (US, UK, Canada, Australia) can enter visa-free for up to 90 days with a valid passport and onward ticket.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit Bora Bora?
faqs.answer: May to October, the dry season, for lower humidity, calmer lagoon waters, and the best underwater visibility. Stable tropical climate with warm temperatures year-round (27-31°C); a drier, cooler season (May-Oct) and a warmer, more humid season with brief showers (Nov-Apr).

faqs.question: What's included in this package?
faqs.answer: Round-trip airfare, accommodation, daily breakfast, airport transfers, daily local transport, and guided tours to the attractions listed in the itinerary. See the Included / Not Included sections for full detail.

faqs.question: Can the itinerary be customized?
faqs.answer: Yes — this itinerary is a starting point. Your travel consultant can add, remove, or reorder activities, extend your stay, or upgrade your hotel category before you book.

faqs.question: What is the cancellation policy?
faqs.answer: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.

faqs.question: Is Bora Bora safe for tourists?
faqs.answer: Bora Bora is rated very safe for tourists, with low rates of violent crime. Usual travel precautions (watch belongings in crowds, use licensed taxis) still apply.

faqs.question: What currency should I bring to Bora Bora?
faqs.answer: The local currency is XPF. Carry some cash for smaller vendors — card acceptance can be inconsistent outside major hotels and malls.

faqs.question: Is this package good for a honeymoon or family trip?
faqs.answer: Yes — Bora Bora is one of our most-booked honeymoon destinations, especially the Overwater villa with direct lagoon access experience.

reviews.author: Lukas Kowalski
reviews.title: Unforgettable trip!
reviews.body: Bora Bora is stunning and this package covered the essentials well, especially Traditional Polynesian dance dinner show. The hotel could have been a little closer to the center but transport was easy.

reviews.author: Anna Müller
reviews.title: Smooth from start to finish
reviews.body: Bora Bora is stunning and this package covered the essentials well, especially Faanui Bay. The hotel could have been a little closer to the center but transport was easy.

reviews.author: Wei Brown
reviews.title: Exceeded our expectations
reviews.body: Booked this as a family of 4 trip and it was well paced. Bloody Mary's restaurant grounds in particular stood out. A couple of the transfers ran a little late but nothing that spoiled the trip.

reviews.author: Noah Rossi
reviews.title: Highly recommend this package
reviews.body: Our trip to Bora Bora was fantastic from start to finish. The hotel was clean and well located, and the guided visit to Le Truck island tour was a real highlight. Airport transfers were on time both ways.
